headlines.title: US judge holds prosecutor in contempt in Charlie Kirk murder case
headlines.snippet: A judge has found a prosecutor in contempt of court for making public statements about a defendant in the Charlie Kirk murder case, which violated media rules. The judge cited comments made to the press as the reason for the ruling.

headlines.title: Venezuela shaken by magnitude 4.9 tremor days after major earthquakes
headlines.snippet: Venezuela experienced a magnitude 4.9 earthquake just days after suffering devastating earthquakes that killed hundreds. The country is still dealing with the aftermath of those earlier, more severe seismic events.

headlines.title: What is the framework agreement signed by Israel and Lebanon?
headlines.snippet: Israel and Lebanon have signed a framework agreement, brokered after months of direct talks facilitated by the United States. This deal comes as Israel continues to occupy Lebanese territory.

headlines.title: Desperate families search for missing loved ones in Venezuela
headlines.snippet: In Venezuela, thousands of desperate families are searching for missing loved ones, posting details online. More than 50,000 people remain unaccounted for following recent devastating earthquakes in the country.

headlines.title: Thousands of migrants flee South Africa ahead of anti-immigrant protests
headlines.snippet: Thousands of migrants are fleeing South Africa amid rising anti-immigrant protests and sentiment. The situation has prompted a large-scale departure of foreign nationals from the country.

headlines.title: JD Vance defends US strikes, warns Iran against escalation
headlines.snippet: JD Vance has defended recent US strikes and issued a warning to Iran against further escalation. He stated that violence would be met with reciprocal violence, emphasizing the importance of adhering to agreements.

headlines.title: Hezbollah supporters block Beirut road to protest Israel-Lebanon agreement
headlines.snippet: Hezbollah supporters have blocked a road in Beirut to protest the newly signed agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Protesters rode motorbikes through the city, some using burning tires to block a road near the parliament.

headlines.title: ‘Washington violated commitments’: IRGC says it targeted US forces in the region
headlines.snippet: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced it targeted US forces in the region, warning of broader retaliation if such actions are repeated. The IRGC stated that the US violated commitments by launching an air strike on the Iranian coast.

headlines.title: Iranian official condemns US attack as ‘reckless violation of ceasefire’
headlines.snippet: An Iranian official has condemned a US attack, labeling it a 'reckless violation of ceasefire.' Ebrahim Azizi stated that the action showed a lack of commitment to negotiation principles and would lead to regret.

headlines.title: Lebanon and Israel sign 'framework agreement' to end war
headlines.snippet: Lebanon and Israel have signed a framework agreement in Washington, D.C., with the United States, following months of talks. The US has pledged $100 million in humanitarian assistance in coordination with the UN for affected communities.

headlines.title: Policemen Rescue Toddler From Destroyed Building in La Guaira
headlines.snippet: In La Guaira, Venezuela, police officers successfully rescued a toddler from a destroyed building. The rescue occurred amidst ongoing efforts to find survivors after recent devastating earthquakes.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Israel, US sign trilateral framework agreement in Washington
headlines.snippet: Lebanon, Israel, and the United States have signed a trilateral framework agreement in Washington. The signing ceremony took place following several rounds of diplomatic talks.

headlines.title: Trump slams Iran drone attack as ‘foolish’ ceasefire violation
headlines.snippet: Trump has described an Iranian drone attack as a 'foolish' violation of the ceasefire. This statement comes amid heightened tensions following the incident in the Hormuz strait.

headlines.title: UAE stresses Hormuz security in rare call with Iran after war tensions
headlines.snippet: The UAE has emphasized the importance of security in the Strait of Hormuz during a rare call with Iran. This discussion occurred after a period of increased war tensions in the region.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it killed seven Hezbollah operatives in south Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli army reported that it killed seven Hezbollah operatives in southern Lebanon. The incident occurred amidst ongoing regional tensions and security operations.

headlines.title: US strikes Iran sites after cargo ship attack, Tehran retaliates
headlines.snippet: US aircraft have bombed targets in Iran following an attack on a cargo ship. Tehran has retaliated, raising concerns about the security of the Strait of Hormuz and the existing ceasefire.

headlines.title: France’s terrorism apology law faces test in pro-Palestine case
headlines.snippet: A Paris court has frozen the trial of Kazib, raising a constitutional question about civil party powers. This decision is seen as a win for the Palestine movement regarding France's terrorism apology law.

headlines.title: Ms Rachel says world has failed Palestinian children at briefing
headlines.snippet: Ms Rachel became emotional during a press briefing concerning Palestinian children, urging world leaders to take action. She apologized to a young girl present at the briefing.

headlines.title: Shia Muslims mark their holiest day in war-battered Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Shia Muslims in Lebanon marked their holiest day, Ashura, with a procession through the ruins of Nabatieh. Mourners defied the destruction caused by the recent conflict in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel deal, warns of civil war
headlines.snippet: Hezbollah has rejected the framework agreement between Lebanon and Israel, warning it could lead to civil war. Meanwhile, Netanyahu affirmed that troops would remain in Lebanon.

headlines.snippet: The army has successfully thwarted an attempt to smuggle drugs using guided balloons. This operation highlights ongoing efforts to combat illicit activities.

headlines.snippet: An attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz has prompted the UN to halt evacuation plans for sailors who were trapped. The incident raises security concerns in the vital waterway.

headlines.title: Lebanon president welcomes French, Italian efforts to form coalition to succeed UNIFIL
headlines.snippet: The President of Lebanon has welcomed efforts by France and Italy to form a coalition that would succeed UNIFIL. This initiative aims to ensure continued stability in the region.

headlines.title: Switzerland, Canada advance as Brazil eye last 32
headlines.snippet: Switzerland and Canada have advanced in the competition, while Brazil is aiming to reach the last 32. The results indicate progress for these teams in the tournament.

headlines.snippet: The idea of a Syrian solution for Lebanon is being characterized as a 'Damascus delusion.' This perspective suggests that such a solution is not viable or realistic.

headlines.snippet: Israeli and Lebanese officials have denied reports of any partial Israeli withdrawal from a 'buffer zone.' Both sides have refuted claims of troop movements or territorial concessions.

headlines.snippet: Rubio, during his tour, stated that any agreement with Iran will ensure the security of Gulf allies. His remarks aimed to reassure regional partners about US commitments.

headlines.snippet: Officials are considering all options if OPEC quotas are not increased, with sources indicating that exiting the organization has been weighed. This suggests potential shifts in oil policy.

headlines.title: White House asks OpenAI to limit its next model release
headlines.snippet: The White House has asked OpenAI to limit the release of its next model, GPT 5.6, due to its advanced capabilities. The request specifies that the release should be restricted to a few government-approved partners.

headlines.snippet: Rubio's comments on the Iran MOU differ from those of Trump and Vance, suggesting a nuanced stance on the agreement. His remarks indicate potential divisions in the US administration's approach.

headlines.snippet: The agreement between the United States and Iran has resulted in a halt to open warfare, marking a significant achievement. This de-escalation is a win that has likely saved lives.

headlines.title: Due to Ebola, Saudi Arabia suspends travel and entry from 3 countries
headlines.snippet: Saudi Arabia has suspended travel and entry from the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, and South Sudan due to concerns about the Ebola virus. This measure is a precautionary step to prevent its spread.

headlines.snippet: Hot and humid weather is expected across northern Egypt, with very hot conditions predicted for the south. Humidity levels are expected to decrease in southern regions.
